display.purpose: Page titles only — one per page ("TTB Label Verifier", "How this tool works").

headline.purpose: Section titles within a page ("Application data", "Government health warning").

title.purpose: Card titles, field-row names, status-banner status word.

body.purpose: All paragraphs, rationale lines, instructional copy. 18px floor; max 70ch on text-heavy pages.

label.purpose: Uppercase metadata — "APPLICATION" / "LABEL" column headers, badge text, table headers.

mono.purpose: Extracted values, normalized values, diff text, warning-statement quotes, regulation citations.

shadows.name: card-lift
shadows.purpose: The one allowed lift. Used on the result card and the application-data form artifact. A 1px hairline plus a soft, low-spread underglow. Never adopted by smaller components.

motion.name: ease-state
motion.purpose: ease-out-quart. Default easing for state transitions (hover, focus, status changes). 120-180ms duration.

motion.name: duration-state
motion.purpose: Larger state transitions (banner appearing, badge swapping).

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The terminal CTA — "Verify label", "Run batch". Rust fill, paper text, 48px tall. One per screen.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; height: 48px; padding: 0 24px; background: oklch(0.55 0.13 40); color: oklch(0.985 0.008 85); border: 1px solid oklch(0.45 0.14 40); border-radius: 8px; font-family: 'Geist', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 18px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: -0.005em; cursor: pointer; transition: background 120ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1), transform 80ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: oklch(0.45 0.14 40); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(0.18 0.012 80); outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(1px); }

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: Secondary affordance — "Try a sample", "Reset". Paper fill, ink text, 1px rule border.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; height: 48px; padding: 0 24px; background: oklch(0.985 0.008 85); color: oklch(0.18 0.012 80); border: 1px solid oklch(0.78 0.012 80); border-radius: 8px; font-family: 'Geist', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 18px; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: background 120ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1); } .ds-btn-outline:hover { background: oklch(0.965 0.012 85); } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(0.18 0.012 80); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Input Field
components.description: Text input — 48px tall (the floor; current code drifts to 32px). Paper fill, rule border, ink text, pencil placeholder.
components.css: .ds-input-wrap { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 6px; font-family: 'Geist', system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-input-label { font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; color: oklch(0.18 0.012 80); } .ds-input-label em { color: oklch(0.42 0.16 25); font-style: normal; margin-left: 4px; } .ds-input { display: block; width: 100%; height: 48px; padding: 0 14px; background: oklch(0.985 0.008 85); color: oklch(0.18 0.012 80); border: 1px solid oklch(0.78 0.012 80); border-radius: 8px; font-family: 'Geist', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 18px; line-height: 1.4; outline: none; transition: border-color 120ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1), box-shadow 120ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1); } .ds-input::placeholder { color: oklch(0.58 0.010 80); } .ds-input:focus-visible { border-color: oklch(0.18 0.012 80); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.55 0.13 40 / 0.20); }

components.name: Status Banner — FAIL
components.description: The marquee verdict header on the result page. Status word in headline weight, subtitle in body, dated meta line. The icon + word + subtitle together carry the status (color alone never).
components.html:
FAIL

One or more required fields do not match, or the government warning is non-compliant.

Verified in 2.4 s
components.css: .ds-banner { display: flex; align-items: flex-start; gap: 16px; padding: 24px; border-radius: 14px; border: 2px solid; font-family: 'Geist', system-ui, sans-serif; box-shadow: 0 1px 0 oklch(0.92 0.01 80), 0 8px 24px -12px oklch(0.18 0.012 80 / 0.10); } .ds-banner-fail { background: oklch(0.965 0.04 25); border-color: oklch(0.78 0.10 25); color: oklch(0.42 0.16 25); } .ds-banner-icon { flex-shrink: 0; width: 40px; height: 40px; margin-top: 2px; } .ds-banner-body { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 4px; } .ds-banner-title { margin: 0; font-size: 30px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: -0.015em; line-height: 1.08; } .ds-banner-subtitle { margin: 0; font-size: 18px; line-height: 1.6; } .ds-banner-meta { margin: 4px 0 0; font-size: 13px; opacity: 0.75; }

components.name: Field Row — Match
components.description: The signature evidentiary row. Three regions: name + rationale (left), application/label values in mono (center), status badge (right). One row per verified field.
components.html:
Brand name

Exact match after whitespace normalization.

APPLICATIONStone's Throw
LABELSTONE'S THROW
Match
components.css: .ds-field-row { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 16rem 1fr auto; gap: 16px; align-items: start; padding: 16px; background: oklch(0.985 0.008 85); border: 1px solid oklch(0.92 0.01 80); border-radius: 8px; font-family: 'Geist', system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-field-meta { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 4px; } .ds-field-name { margin: 0; font-size: 18px; font-weight: 600; color: oklch(0.18 0.012 80); } .ds-field-rationale { margin: 0; font-size: 15px; line-height: 1.5; color: oklch(0.42 0.011 80); } .ds-field-values { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; gap: 12px; } .ds-field-vlabel { display: block; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.06em; text-transform: uppercase; color: oklch(0.58 0.010 80); margin-bottom: 4px; } .ds-field-value { display: block; font-family: 'Geist Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 15px; color: oklch(0.18 0.012 80); background: oklch(0.965 0.012 85); padding: 6px 8px; border-radius: 4px; } .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; padding: 4px 10px; border-radius: 4px; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.06em; text-transform: uppercase; height: fit-content; } .ds-badge svg { width: 14px; height: 14px; } .ds-badge-pass { background: oklch(0.965 0.035 145); color: oklch(0.42 0.10 145); }

components.name: Warning Red-line
components.description: The Government Health Warning verification — canonical text vs extracted text, with diff overlay. The marked-up filing's most consequential artifact.
components.html:
Government health warning

Non-compliant

CANONICAL (27 CFR 16.21)

GOVERNMENT WARNING: According to the Surgeon General, women should not drink alcoholic beverages during pregnancy because of the risk of birth defects.

READ FROM LABEL

Government Warning: According to the Surgeon General, women should not drink alcoholic beverages during pregnancy because of the risk of birth defects.

Header is ALL CAPSFailed
components.css: .ds-redline { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 12px; padding: 20px; border: 1px solid oklch(0.92 0.01 80); border-radius: 8px; background: oklch(0.985 0.008 85); font-family: 'Geist', system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-redline-header { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; gap: 8px; } .ds-redline-title { margin: 0; font-size: 18px; font-weight: 600; color: oklch(0.18 0.012 80); } .ds-redline-pill { padding: 4px 12px; border-radius: 9999px; border: 1px solid; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 500; } .ds-redline-pill-fail { background: oklch(0.965 0.04 25); border-color: oklch(0.78 0.10 25); color: oklch(0.42 0.16 25); } .ds-redline-grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; gap: 16px; } .ds-redline-vlabel { margin: 0 0 6px; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.06em; text-transform: uppercase; color: oklch(0.58 0.010 80); font-family: 'Geist', system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-redline-block { margin: 0; padding: 12px; background: oklch(0.965 0.012 85); border: 1px solid oklch(0.92 0.01 80); border-radius: 6px; font-family: 'Geist Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 14px; line-height: 1.55; color: oklch(0.18 0.012 80); } .ds-redline-flag { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; margin: 0; font-size: 15px; color: oklch(0.18 0.012 80); } .ds-redline-dot { display: inline-block; width: 10px; height: 10px; border-radius: 9999px; flex-shrink: 0; } .ds-redline-dot-fail { background: oklch(0.42 0.16 25); } .ds-redline-flag-fail { color: oklch(0.42 0.16 25); font-weight: 500; }

components.name: Navigation
components.description: Horizontal page nav — Single | Batch | About. 48px-tall ghost links; active state inverts to ink fill / paper text.
components.html: SingleBatchAbout
components.css: .ds-nav { display: inline-flex; gap: 4px; font-family: 'Geist', system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-nav-link { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; min-width: 48px; height: 48px; padding: 0 16px; border-radius: 6px; font-size: 18px; font-weight: 500; color: oklch(0.18 0.012 80); text-decoration: none; transition: background 120ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1); } .ds-nav-link:hover { background: oklch(0.965 0.012 85); } .ds-nav-link:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(0.18 0.012 80); outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-nav-link-active { background: oklch(0.18 0.012 80); color: oklch(0.985 0.008 85); }

narrative.overview:
This is a tool that should look like a printed federal filing being marked up in real time — not a SaaS dashboard, not a Linear-clone, not a 'modernized' rebrand of a government form. The reviewer is doing the same job they've done for years (compare label to application, flag mismatches, hand the file back), and the interface is their evidence kit: a clean cream sheet, ink-black type, and a single rust-red pen for citations and red-lines. The AI's job is to read; the reviewer's job is to decide. The visual system enforces that hierarchy at every level.

The discipline borrowed from editorial publishing is real — typographic hierarchy, dense-but-legible body text, mono-set citations, dated artifacts — but it is executed in sans-serif and monospace, never serifs. Cream + ink + rust is the entire palette of voice; status colors (forest-green, library-amber, archive-red) are quiet, library-stamp-y, never candy. Borders carry separation. Shadows are reserved for 'this artifact is the result page, elevated above the form' — and used exactly once.
keyCharacteristics: Cream-paper surface; ink-black text; rust accent used as the editor's red pen.
Sans-serif throughout; monospace for diffs, normalized values, and citations.
18px body floor; 48px minimum touch height; status communicated by color + icon + text simultaneously.
Borders carry layout; shadows are reserved (one allowed: the result-card lift).
Restrained motion: state changes only, no choreography, no decoration.
Editorial discipline (hierarchy, citations, red-lines) executed in a sans type system.

rules.name: The One Pen Rule
rules.body: Rust is the editor's pen, and it is the only accent voice in the system. It appears on the primary CTA, red-line annotation underlines, and citation links. Never used as a fill, never gradient, never combined with another saturated accent.

rules.name: The Cream Floor Rule
rules.body: Page surface is paper, never #fff. Card and inset surface is paper or bone, never a third lighter variant invented at the component level. If a surface needs to feel lifted, it gets a 1px ledger border and one shadow-card — not a brighter fill.

rules.name: The Status-Trio Rule
rules.body: Every status color must appear with a matching icon and a matching text label. A green dot alone, a red border alone, an amber fill alone — all forbidden. Color-blind users must read the same verdict.

rules.name: The 18px Floor Rule
rules.body: Body type never falls below 18px. The persona is a 73-year-old reviewer in office light; 14px utility text exists for badges and labels only, and never carries semantic content (it always restates a neighboring 18px line).

rules.name: The Two-Voice Rule
rules.body: The system has exactly two type voices: Geist (the narrator) and Geist Mono (the witness). Mono is reserved for things that the reviewer would set in monospace if they were marking up the filing by hand — extracted text, the canonical regulation, the diff. Don't use mono decoratively.

rules.name: The Citation Rule
rules.body: Regulation references (e.g. 27 CFR 16.21) are always typeset in Mono and may carry a Rust underline. They are the only place rust appears in body copy.

rules.name: The Flat Page Rule
rules.body: The default surface is flat. If a component wants a shadow, the answer is 'use a border instead' — until you can name a specific reason it needs to lift off the page. There is exactly one named lift in the system (shadow-card); inventing more is forbidden.

rules.name: The Border-Carries-Layout Rule
rules.body: Visual separation between regions is always carried by a 1px ledger or rule border, never by a tonal step. A 'subtle background variation' between regions is forbidden — it is illegible at 73-year-old contrast tolerances and reads as 'AI made it.'

dos: Do use paper (oklch(0.985 0.008 85)) for every page surface. Never #fff.
Do set body type at 18px and headings at ≥1.25 ratio steps. The 18px floor is a promise to a 73-year-old reviewer.
Do make every interactive target ≥48px tall, including inputs. The current h-8 (32px) inputs from components/ui/input.tsx are a drift and must be migrated to 48px.
Do show the receipts. Every field row carries application value, label value, and the rationale that produced the verdict — in mono, side-by-side.
Do set citations (e.g. 27 CFR 16.21) in Geist Mono and let them carry a rust underline. That is the citation rule.
Do use color + icon + text together for every status. A reviewer with red-green color blindness must read the same verdict.
Do keep motion to state-change feedback (120-180ms ease-out). Borders lighten, surfaces tint, focus rings appear. Nothing translates more than 1px. Nothing scales. Nothing animates entrances.
donts: Don't use #000 or #fff anywhere. Pure black is dead; pure white is cold. Every neutral is tinted toward the warm spine of the palette.
Don't use Times New Roman, Georgia, or any serif body type. Editorial discipline in sans-serif — never editorial costume in serifs.

Don't introduce a third accent color. Rust is the editor's pen and it is the only one. Violet, blue, teal, purple — all forbidden as accents. The current violet-300 / violet-50 / violet-900 'Reviewed by Sonnet' badge is a drift and must migrate to a neutral mark.
Don't use side-stripe borders (border-left: 4px solid) on cards, callouts, or alerts. Forbidden. Use a full border + status-tint surface instead.
Don't wrap content in nested cards. Nested cards are always wrong.
Don't use shadows on individual list rows or buttons. Shadow is reserved for the result and form artifacts only — the named shadow-card lift.
Don't ship gradient text or background-clip: text decorative type. Forbidden. Hierarchy is carried by weight and size, not by color spectacle.
Don't use sparkle icons decoratively to signal 'AI.' We have a literal 'Reviewed by Sonnet' affordance for that; decorative sparkle icons are the AI-bro SaaS tell.
Don't style the result with confetti, 'Looks great!' toasts, or exclamation-pointed copy. Voice is plainspoken: 'Brand name matches' not 'Looks like a match!'
Don't put a 'Powered by AI' badge anywhere in the product.
Don't introduce a dark mode without revisiting PRODUCT.md. The persona is a TTB reviewer in office light. The current .dark block in globals.css is unused legacy and should be removed.
Don't use raw Tailwind palette names (green-700, red-200, slate-50, violet-300) in new code. Migrate to the named tokens above.
